title: Slugs, Permalinks, Redirects, Canonicals, and Route Manifests · Traceback Repair Bay
summary: A preserved build trace contains one plausible source, path, encoding, metadata, parser, renderer, template, URL, asset, accessibility, security, cache, package, deployment, or release defect; locate the first failed boundary before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in collision-free versioned route and redirect manifest and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal de…
objectives: Separate stable content identity from mutable title, source path, output path, display label, and public URL.
Define Unicode normalization, transliteration or preservation, case, separators, reserved names, empties, length, and collision behavior.
Select explicit or derived permalinks, trailing-slash convention, date behavior, language prefix, and base path with migration evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Inspect the failure record · Debug Stable Route Identity
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug publishing team handles case 2074 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to produce stable collision-free routes, canonicals, and an acyclic redirect graph; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate stable content identity from mutable title, source path, output path, display label, and public URL.

Inspect the Stable Route Identity trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

steps.instruction:
Append a runnable pure-Python function headed "# Evidence: ssg-canonical-origin-vc9gob" that uses case 1539 to produce stable collision-free routes, canonicals, and an acyclic redirect graph. Keep its defaults runnable, assert an invariant, and return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Canonical Origin into observable performance evidence. For Static-site publishing and Python Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Canonical Origin, keep Canonical Origin connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-canonical-origin-vc9gob
# Competency: ssg-canonical-origin.
# Case 1539: a compromised-content recovery rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects.
# Changed case: rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence.
def evidence_ssg_canonical_origin_vc9gob(documents: int = 11, routes: int = 11, redirects: int = 3, collisions: int = 0, redirect_loops: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_vc9gob = "vc9gob-bog9cv-1539"
    assert evidence_variant_vc9gob
    evidence_axes_vc9gob = (
        "readervc9gob",
        "source1539",
        "parsebog9cv",
        "routevc9gob1539",
        "artifact1539vc9gob",
        "recoverybog9cv1539",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_vc9gob)) == 6
    if documents < 1 or routes < 0:
        return False, routes, "route-count"
    if routes != documents:
        return False, routes, "route-coverage"
    if redirects < 0 or collisions > 0:
        return False, collisions, "route-collision"
    if redirect_loops > 0:
        return False, redirect_loops, "redirect-loop"
    assert routes == documents
    return True, routes, "stable-route-manifest"
